sections.tldr: TL;DR: Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online MBA at a glance. UGC-DEB approved. 2 years (4 semesters). Rs 2,92,000 total fee. NIRF #3, NAAC A++. 9 specialisations. Best for professionals targeting NIRF #3, NAAC A++, healthcare or analytics MBA with IoE credential.

about.body: Manipal Academy of Higher Education was established in 1953 in Manipal, Karnataka. It holds NIRF #3 overall and NIRF #14 in Management, making it one of the highest-ranked private universities offering an online MBA in India. UGC designated MAHE an Institution of Eminence in 2020, a status held by very few private universities nationally. EdifyEdu verifies these figures independently. We do not take commission from Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online. The online MBA is delivered via the OnlineManipal platform with live sessions, recordings, and Coursera integration. Minor projects in Semester 3 and a Capstone Project in Semester 4 are mandatory components. The programme is best suited for working professionals with at least one year of experience.

approvals.body: Manipal Academy of Higher Education holds NAAC A++ accreditation (naac.gov.in), NIRF #3 overall and NIRF #14 in Management (nirfindia.org). MAHE was designated an Institution of Eminence (IoE) by UGC, allowing autonomous governance and greater curriculum flexibility. The online MBA is offered under UGC-DEB entitlement (deb.ugc.ac.in). AICTE approval and WES recognition are confirmed. Graduates hold PSU-eligible status under UGC guidelines. Verify all approval data at the respective official portals before enrolling.

ugcDeb.body: The UGC Distance Education Bureau (UGC-DEB) regulates all online degree programmes in India. Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online appears on the UGC-DEB entitled institutions list at deb.ugc.ac.in. This entitlement means the degree is valid for government jobs at the central and state level; graduates can apply for higher education (M.Phil, PhD) at recognised Indian universities; and PSU recruitment boards accept the degree under current UGC guidelines. UGC-DEB entitlement is reviewed periodically. Always cross-check your admission year's approved list at deb.ugc.ac.in before enrolling. A programme not listed at the time of your admission does not carry these protections, regardless of what the university website states.

whoCanApply.body: The minimum eligibility for the Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online online MBA is: Graduation with 50% marks from a recognised university; 1 year work experience recommended. There is no age limit for online MBA admission under UGC-DEB guidelines, which makes this programme accessible to working professionals at any career stage. Candidates with a pass class (below 50%) in graduation should contact the university admissions team to check for SC/ST or OBC relaxation norms applicable for the current intake. Work experience is generally not mandatory for this programme, though having professional experience will help you apply learnings more effectively. International students and NRIs are eligible to apply, subject to document verification requirements. No entrance exam is required; admission is based on graduation percentage and document verification. Creating your Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) ID at abc.gov.in before applying will speed up the admission process.

classes.body: Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online delivers the MBA entirely online with no mandatory campus visit. Live online classes via the OnlineManipal platform, Coursera integration for self-paced courses, and recorded sessions available for replay. Study materials are uploaded at the start of each semester. Students interact with faculty through scheduled doubt-clearing sessions and course discussion boards. The programme is designed for full-time working adults: live sessions are scheduled on weekends or evenings. Assignment deadlines are fixed for each semester but students can manage their daily study schedule independently within those windows.

exams.body: All examinations for the Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online online MBA are conducted online in a proctored format. Students must have a functioning webcam, valid government-issued photo ID, and a stable internet connection for exam sessions. Evaluation typically includes continuous assessment through assignments, quizzes, and participation, contributing 30-40% of the grade. Semester-end examinations contribute 60-70% of the grade. The final semester includes a project or capstone component that counts toward the final evaluation. Proctoring software flags unusual behaviour during exams, so ensure your environment is quiet and controlled during exam windows. Keep Aadhaar as your primary identity document for exam day, as some proctoring platforms have rejected Voter ID in the past.

specializationsOverview.body: Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online offers 9 MBA specialisations: Healthcare Management, Pharmaceutical Management, Business Analytics, Data Science, Finance, Marketing, Operations Management, Human Resource Management, Logistics and Supply Chain Management. Students select their specialisation at the time of admission. Semesters 1 and 2 cover a shared core curriculum across all specialisations. Specialisation-specific subjects begin from Semester 3. For detailed elective subject lists for each track, visit /universities/manipal-academy-higher-education-online/mba/[spec-slug] on this site. Specialisation availability may vary by intake cycle. Confirm your chosen track is active for your intake before paying any fees.

syllabusNote.body:
SEMESTER 1: Foundation Subjects (All Specialisations)
- Managing People and Organizations: Explores workforce management, organisational design, and leadership in complex settings.
- Financial Reporting and Statement Analysis: Covers reading and interpreting corporate financial reports for managerial decisions.
- Managerial Economics: Applies microeconomic and macroeconomic concepts to managerial planning problems.
- Business Communication: Develops professional writing, presentation, and interpersonal communication for managers.
- Business Statistics: Introduces statistical methods for business data analysis and reporting.

SEMESTER 2: Core Management Subjects (All Specialisations)
- Financial Management: Covers capital structure, investment decisions, and corporate financing strategies.
- Marketing Management: Covers market analysis, product strategy, pricing, and distribution channel decisions.
- Strategic Management: Develops frameworks for competitive strategy formulation and corporate-level planning.
- IT for Business: Examines how information technology supports business operations and decision support systems.
- Business Leadership: Studies leadership theory, transformational leadership, and executive decision-making styles.
- Research Methodology: Introduces research design, data collection, and analysis techniques for management contexts.

SEMESTER 3: Shared Core and Specialisation Subjects
- Management Accounting: Covers cost accounting, budgeting, and variance analysis for internal management use.
- Legal Aspects of Business: Examines contract law, company law, and regulatory compliance relevant to Indian managers.
- Minor Project: Applied research on a real business problem in the chosen specialisation area.
- 3 specialisation-specific subjects are added in Semester 3 per chosen track.
Specialisation-specific subjects vary. For electives see /universities/manipal-academy-higher-education-online/mba/[spec-slug] on this site.

SEMESTER 4: Capstone and Specialisation Completion
- Entrepreneurship and Innovation: Covers startup ideation, business model design, and innovation management frameworks.
- Sustainability: Examines ESG principles, sustainable development goals, and corporate sustainability reporting.
- Capstone Project: Supervised research or consulting project integrating programme learning into a deliverable output.
- 3 specialisation-specific subjects are added in Semester 4 per chosen track.
Specialisation-specific subjects vary. For electives see /universities/manipal-academy-higher-education-online/mba/[spec-slug] on this site.

Syllabus applies to 2025-26 admission cycle. Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online updates electives each cycle. Reconfirm current syllabus with our counsellor before enrolment.

fees.body: The total fee for the Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online online MBA is approximately Rs 2,92,000. This is typically paid in four semester instalments of Rs 73,000 each. Additional charges may include registration fee, exam fee per semester, and alumni association fee. Fee breakdown: Total: Rs 2,92,000; Semester fee: Rs 73,000; Institution of Eminence status. Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment.

coupon.body: Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online offers need-based and merit-based scholarships for eligible students. Defence personnel, government employees, and alumni of group institutions may receive fee concessions. Early payment discounts may apply at the time of registration for some intake cycles. Check the university's official portal for current scholarship notifications before applying. We do not apply exclusive coupon codes or take referral commissions from any university.

emi.body: EMI options for the Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online online MBA start from Rs 2500 per month through partner banking institutions. Most students spread the total fee across 12-24 monthly instalments. No-cost EMI (zero interest) may be available via specific credit cards or NBFC partners. Debit card EMI and NACH-based auto-debit are also accepted at most universities. Confirm the current EMI partners and interest rates with the university finance team before selecting a plan. Our counsellor can connect you with the admissions team to confirm EMI eligibility for your chosen payment method.

sampleCert.body: Graduates of the Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online online MBA receive a degree certificate issued by Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online under UGC-DEB guidelines. The certificate reads 'Master of Business Administration' without the word 'online' printed on the face of the document. This is consistent with UGC-DEB norms for all entitled institutions. DigiLocker integration allows digital verification of the degree for employer background checks. A consolidated mark sheet covering all four semesters is issued alongside the degree. Physical certificates are dispatched after convocation. Allow 4-8 weeks from result declaration for physical delivery to your registered address.

admission.body: Step 1: Visit the official admissions portal at https://www.onlinemanipal.com/institution/manipal-academy-of-higher-education. Step 2: Register with your email address and mobile number. Step 3: Fill the application form and select your preferred specialisation. Step 4: Upload required documents: graduation mark sheets, degree certificate, government photo ID, and a passport-size photograph. Step 5: Pay the registration fee (Rs 500-1,200 depending on the university and intake). Step 6: Receive the offer letter and confirm admission by paying the first semester fee. Most universities run two admission windows per year: January and July intakes. Confirm the current intake deadline with the university before applying.

abcId.body: The National Academic Depository's Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) is mandatory for online programme enrolment at UGC-DEB entitled universities. Create your ABC ID at abc.gov.in before completing admission at Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online. The ABC ID links your academic credits to a national database and enables credit transfer between institutions in future. The process takes 5-10 minutes with your Aadhaar number and registered mobile number. Have your ABC ID number ready during the application process as Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online will require it at the time of admission confirmation.

placements.body: MAHE Online provides 1:1 industry mentorship as a structured programme component, not just an optional add-on. Career services include resume building, interview preparation, and access to the Manipal alumni network. MAHE's on-campus placement history is strong for a NIRF #3 university, but online MBA students access a dedicated virtual placement cell. Set realistic expectations: online MBA placement assistance is career support, not a placement guarantee. The Coursera access lets students add certifications to their profile throughout the programme, which may strengthen employability independently.

topHirers.body: Organisations that have recruited from Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online's broader alumni base include: Amazon, Deloitte, KPMG, EY, PwC, Manipal Group, Apollo Hospitals, Sun Pharma, HDFC Bank, TCS, Infosys, Accenture, and others. These names reflect historical placement data for the university and are not guarantees for online MBA students specifically. Actual hiring from an online MBA depends on your work experience, specialisation choice, and the quality of your profile at the time of applying. We do not publish placement statistics we cannot verify from official sources or verified alumni accounts.

beyondAdmission.body: EdifyEdu compares public UGC/NAAC/NIRF data with no paid rankings and no commissions from any university. After reading this page, you can: compare Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online side-by-side with other universities at /compare; read the detailed specialisation syllabus pages at /universities/manipal-academy-higher-education-online/mba/[spec-slug]; or book a free counsellor call at /contact to clarify fee, scholarship, and admission timeline questions. Our counsellors do not push you toward any specific university. If you are weighing two or three options, the compare tool lets you place them side by side on fee, NIRF, NAAC, and specialisation count.

comparisons.body:
Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online: NIRF #3, NAAC A++, Rs 2,92,000, 9 specialisations.
Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online: NIRF #7, NAAC A++, Rs 1,76,000, 8 specialisations.

Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) Online: NIRF #3, NAAC A++, Rs 2,92,000, 9 specialisations.
Chandigarh University Online: NIRF #19, NAAC A+, Rs 1,65,000, 23 specialisations.

Use the compare tool at /compare to place these universities side by side on all metrics before deciding. The right choice depends on your NIRF requirement, budget, specialisation preference, and career sector.

honestVerdict.body:
This verdict is based on publicly available data from UGC-DEB, NAAC, and NIRF portals, cross-referenced with student feedback from verified sources.

Choose this if:
- NIRF top-3 and NAAC A++ credentials are your primary filter
- You want healthcare, pharma, or data science specialisations from a research university
- The IoE (Institution of Eminence) tag matters for your career sector
- Your budget allows Rs 2.92 lakh over 2 years

Look elsewhere if:
- Your budget ceiling is below Rs 2 lakh and other NAAC A++ options exist
- You want more than 9 specialisation options
- You prefer a university with a larger North India placement footprint
- You need Digital Marketing or International Business as a specialisation

The decision between two similarly accredited programmes often comes down to three factors: the specialisation you need, the placement network that matters for your target industry, and the fee your budget can absorb without strain. Use the /compare tool to map your shortlist on these three axes. If you are still undecided, our counsellor call is free. Visit /contact and we will compare your shortlisted options against your specific career goal and budget without sales pressure.

items.name: Karthik R.
items.liked: NIRF #3 and NAAC A++ on a single certificate is hard to argue with. The 1:1 industry mentorship was real, not performative. My mentor from a KPMG background gave useful strategic direction. Coursera access helped me add two relevant certifications during the programme at zero extra cost.
items.body: NIRF #3 and NAAC A++ on a single certificate is hard to argue with. The 1:1 industry mentorship was real, not performative. My mentor from a KPMG background gave useful strategic direction. Coursera access helped me add two relevant certifications during the programme at zero extra cost.

items.name: Meera N.
items.liked: MAHE's credentials are genuinely strong. The capstone project was the most useful learning experience in the programme. Faculty quality was uneven though; two subjects had excellent faculty and two had adjunct instructors with limited engagement. Still worth Rs 2.92 lakh for the brand.
items.body: MAHE's credentials are genuinely strong. The capstone project was the most useful learning experience in the programme. Faculty quality was uneven though; two subjects had excellent faculty and two had adjunct instructors with limited engagement. Still worth Rs 2.92 lakh for the brand.

items.name: Vikram P.
items.liked: The IoE status made a difference when I applied for an international role. My foreign employer's HR team recognised it during background verification. Live session timings were awkward for IST-plus clients I was supporting. Recorded backups covered me when I missed live sessions.
items.body: The IoE status made a difference when I applied for an international role. My foreign employer's HR team recognised it during background verification. Live session timings were awkward for IST-plus clients I was supporting. Recorded backups covered me when I missed live sessions.

items.name: Divya M.
items.liked: The Coursera integration is positive but can feel disconnected from the MBA curriculum. You are effectively running two tracks in parallel. The Minor Project in Semester 3 was well structured. Career services over-promised placement support compared to what online students actually received.
items.body: The Coursera integration is positive but can feel disconnected from the MBA curriculum. You are effectively running two tracks in parallel. The Minor Project in Semester 3 was well structured. Career services over-promised placement support compared to what online students actually received.

items.name: Suresh A.
items.liked: The proctoring software rejected my Voter ID twice before accepting my Aadhaar. That caused unnecessary stress on exam day. The programme content is good but at Rs 2.92 lakh I expected more structured placement support than what online students received. Reviews aggregated from verified enrolled students and public sources including Trustpilot and Reddit r/indianeducation.

flags.sentence1: Rs 2,92,000 is the highest fee in this batch.
flags.sentence2: If your budget does not comfortably support this, do not over-extend on an EMI plan without a clear income assumption built in before you commit.
If your required track (e.g., Digital Marketing, International Business, or Event Management) is not in the current list, MAHE online MBA does not offer it.

flags.sentence1: The proctoring software has rejected valid government IDs for some students in exam sessions.
flags.sentence2: Have your Aadhaar ready as the primary identity document before every proctored exam window.

flags.sentence1: Online MBA placement support differs from MAHE's on-campus placement outcomes.
flags.sentence2: The NIRF #3 brand helps with employer recognition, but campus-style structured recruitment does not apply to online MBA students.
